title: Impossibile accedere ad un sito solo con Firefox
content:
Buonasera,

Da un po di tempo provo ad accedere ad uno specifico sito web, per il quale ho sottoscritto un abbonamento. Ma ogni volta che inserisco le credenziali ottengo una pagina di errore con la seguente dicitura:

{ 

 "callId": "019dcbdc4bb67b4d8cbc9d9244293f0a",  "errorCode": 403005,  "errorDetails": "Unauthorized user",  "errorMessage": "Unauthorized user",  "apiVersion": 2,  "statusCode": 403,  "statusReason": "Forbidden",  "time": "2026-04-26T22:15:05.407Z"

}

All'inizio riuscivo ad accedere al sito senza alcun problema usando Firefox, ma da qualche tempo non è più possibile. Se provo ad accedere allo stesso sito usando altri browser tutto sembra andare bene. Ho provato a contattare l'assistenza del sito web per ottenere chiarimenti ma non ho ottenuto alcun risultato.

Cosa può essere accaduto?

title: Why is Youtube not loading videos correctly seemingly at random?
content:
I have noticed that since around Friday, April 24th, 2026, that Youtube videos will sometimes refuse to load properly. The page for the video will come up, but buttons on the page will not load, and nor will the video. This started happening around mid-day, when prior to it, things had been fine. At first I thought it might be tied into the 'ambient mode' option on Youtube itself., But even after shutting that off, I found it was still happening at ranodm.

Is this something new Yotube is doing in their continued (idiotic) fight against ad blockers, or is there an actual glitch in the browser or, more likely, a glitch in Youtube? Like I said, this seems to occur at random and I can't trace the cause for it. I'll include a screenshot of what I'm seeing. But if anyone knows what's happening here, I'd appreciate it.

I should add that I am on Windows 11 and using the new version 150.0 for Firefox. (Congrats on that, by the way.)

I'll be honest, I keep hearing some other browser can block youtube ads innately. (Brave, I think it was called?) and while the idea is cool, I'm not giving up a browser I've used for over 20 years for it. If Firefox can find a way to implement a similar means of blocking Youtube ads innately, I;d love it. But for now, I just wanna know what the devil is happening with the site. Can anyone help?
creator: Marc7

title: Issues with Mozilla VPN nstalled on my Windows 11 PC with two user accounts
content:
I've used Mozilla VPN for years. I have the regular paid subscription and it is installed on a Windows 11 PC that has two user accounts (one for Gaming, one for Work). My issue is that when I log off on one user and log on to the other user account, Mozilla VPN requires a fresh installation (as if it's never been installed before). This happens every time I change from one of my user accounts to the other, which during the course of a day I do quite frequently.

To re-cap: the VPN works perfectly on my gaming user account and I have it set to automatically start with windows when I boot up. All is good and it works great. Then when I switch to my Work user account it requires that I reinstall. So I go through the process of reinstalling the software...and it works great...until I switch back to my other account. Then I have to go through exactly the same re-install process.

It never used to be this way. I always used to be able to sign off on one user account and sign in on the other, and the VPN would work fine.

Any help with this would definitely be appreciated.
creator: sevans_nv
